article_meta.title: After some 150 students walked out of Redlands schools early this month in support of immigrants, they were dealt an une

reasoning: Mixed quality: one clear attribution violation, several questionable flags, missed at least one author-level bias instance, and all bias type labels are outside the allowed set.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Flags a paraphrased, clearly attributed source sentiment (“student activists … said they are willing to face consequences…”) as author bias, violating the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Several flagged passages are neutral or context-setting and not clear bias (e.g., highlighting supportive districts; ‘align with the students’ criticism’).
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Misses at least one notable author-level bias phrase like “unexpected consequence,” and relies little on clearer instances such as subjective comparative framing beyond the first item.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: All labels used (e.g., Framing Bias, Selection Bias, Labeling Bias, Sympathetic Tone, Negative Association) are not in the allowed set, constituting systematic type errors.
